name: French B1 Grammar
description: CEFR B1 French grammar, scoped from the Référentiel B1 (Beacco et al., Didier). Fill in the blank.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il faut que je {___} plus lentement.
(I need to speak more slowly.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I need to speak more slowly.
facts.explanation: parle — que je form of parler. Formation: nous-form of present indicative (parlons) minus -ons → parl-, then add subjonctif ending -e. Subjonctif endings: -e, -es, -e, -ions, -iez, -ent.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je veux que tu {___} avec le professeur.
(I want you to speak with the teacher.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I want you to speak with the teacher.
facts.explanation: parles — que tu form of parler. Subjonctif stem: parl- (parlons minus -ons) + ending -es for tu.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il est important que nous {___} français en classe.
(It's important that we speak French in class.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It's important that we speak French in class.
facts.explanation: parlions — que nous form of parler. Subjonctif nous-form: parl- + -ions. Identical spelling to the imparfait nous-form, but triggered by subjonctif context (il est important que...).

facts.quizQuestion:
Je préfère que vous {___} plus fort.
(I prefer that you speak louder.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I prefer that you speak louder.
facts.explanation: parliez — que vous form of parler. Subjonctif stem: parl- + -iez.

facts.quizQuestion:
C'est dommage qu'ils ne {___} pas anglais.
(It's a shame that they don't speak English.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It's a shame that they don't speak English.
facts.explanation: parlent — qu'ils form of parler. Subjonctif ils-form: parl- + -ent. For regular -er verbs this is identical to the present indicative ils-form.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il vaut mieux que je {___} avant le départ.
(It's better that I eat before leaving.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It's better that I eat before leaving.
facts.explanation: mange — que je form of manger. Subjonctif stem: mang- (mangeons minus -eons → mang-) + -e. The -e- spelling of mangeons (avoids hard -g-) disappears in the subjonctif.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il est nécessaire que nous {___} ensemble ce soir.
(It's necessary that we eat together tonight.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It's necessary that we eat together tonight.
facts.explanation: mangions — que nous form of manger. Subjonctif nous-form: mang- + -ions. Unlike the indicative (mangeons), the -e- is dropped here.

facts.quizQuestion:
J'aimerais que tu {___} ce film avec moi.
(I'd like you to watch this film with me.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I'd like you to watch this film with me.
facts.explanation: regardes — que tu form of regarder. Subjonctif stem: regard- (regardons minus -ons) + -es.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il propose que nous {___} cette émission ensemble.
(He suggests that we watch this programme together.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He suggests that we watch this programme together.
facts.explanation: regardions — que nous form of regarder. Subjonctif stem: regard- + -ions.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je veux qu'il {___} son travail.
(I want him to enjoy his work.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I want him to enjoy his work.
facts.explanation: aime — qu'il form of aimer. Subjonctif stem: aim- (aimons minus -ons) + -e.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il est bon que nous {___} notre métier.
(It's good that we enjoy our work.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It's good that we enjoy our work.
facts.explanation: aimions — que nous form of aimer. Subjonctif stem: aim- + -ions.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il vaut mieux que je {___} davantage.
(It's better that I work more.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It's better that I work more.
facts.explanation: travaille — que je form of travailler. Subjonctif stem: travaill- (travaillons minus -ons) + -e.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il faut que vous {___} en équipe.
(You need to work as a team.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: You need to work as a team.
facts.explanation: travailliez — que vous form of travailler. Subjonctif stem: travaill- + -iez.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il est important qu'ils {___} régulièrement.
(It's important that they study regularly.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It's important that they study regularly.
facts.explanation: étudient — qu'ils form of étudier. Subjonctif stem: étudi- (étudions minus -ons) + -ent.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il vaut mieux que nous {___} ce sujet ensemble.
(It's better that we study this subject together.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It's better that we study this subject together.
facts.explanation: étudiions — que nous form of étudier. Subjonctif stem: étudi- + -ions = étudiions (double-i: stem ends in -i, suffix begins with -i).

facts.quizQuestion:
Il faut que je {___} ce rapport avant midi.
(I need to finish this report before noon.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I need to finish this report before noon.
facts.explanation: finisse — que je form of finir. Formation: nous-form (finissons) minus -ons → finiss-, then add -e. The -iss- infix appears throughout the whole subjonctif paradigm for finir-type verbs.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je veux que tu {___} tes devoirs avant de sortir.
(I want you to finish your homework before going out.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I want you to finish your homework before going out.
facts.explanation: finisses — que tu form of finir. Subjonctif stem: finiss- + -es.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il est nécessaire que nous {___} à l'heure.
(It's necessary that we finish on time.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It's necessary that we finish on time.
facts.explanation: finissions — que nous form of finir. Subjonctif stem: finiss- + -ions.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je préfère que vous {___} ce projet cette semaine.
(I prefer that you finish this project this week.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I prefer that you finish this project this week.
facts.explanation: finissiez — que vous form of finir. Subjonctif stem: finiss- + -iez.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il est important qu'ils {___} leur travail correctement.
(It's important that they finish their work correctly.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It's important that they finish their work correctly.
facts.explanation: finissent — qu'ils form of finir. Subjonctif stem: finiss- + -ent.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il faut que je {___} une option avant demain.
(I need to choose an option before tomorrow.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I need to choose an option before tomorrow.
facts.explanation: choisisse — que je form of choisir. Formation: nous-form (choisissons) minus -ons → choisiss-, then add -e.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il vaut mieux que nous {___} ensemble.
(It's better that we choose together.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It's better that we choose together.
facts.explanation: choisissions — que nous form of choisir. Subjonctif stem: choisiss- + -ions.

facts.quizQuestion:
J'espère que tu {___} à tes examens.
(I hope you succeed in your exams.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I hope you succeed in your exams.
facts.explanation: réussisses — que tu form of réussir. Formation: nous-form (réussissons) minus -ons → réussiss-, then add -es.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il est important qu'ils {___} dans la vie.
(It's important that they succeed in life.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It's important that they succeed in life.
facts.explanation: réussissent — qu'ils form of réussir. Subjonctif stem: réussiss- + -ent.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il faut que je {___} le train de sept heures.
(I need to take the seven o'clock train.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I need to take the seven o'clock train.
facts.explanation: prenne — que je form of prendre. Formation: nous-form (prenons) minus -ons → pren-, then add -ne for je/il/elle. The singular and ils forms use the doubled stem prenn-.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je veux que tu {___} soin de toi.
(I want you to take care of yourself.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I want you to take care of yourself.
facts.explanation: prennes — que tu form of prendre. Subjonctif stem for je/tu/il/ils: prenn- (doubled-n) + -es for tu.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il est important que nous {___} une décision rapidement.
(It's important that we make a decision quickly.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It's important that we make a decision quickly.
facts.explanation: prenions — que nous form of prendre. Nous/vous forms use single-n stem pren-: pren- + -ions.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je préfère que vous {___} l'autobus.
(I prefer that you take the bus.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I prefer that you take the bus.
facts.explanation: preniez — que vous form of prendre. Subjonctif stem: pren- + -iez.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il faut qu'ils {___} leurs responsabilités au sérieux.
(They need to take their responsibilities seriously.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: They need to take their responsibilities seriously.
facts.explanation: prennent — qu'ils form of prendre. Subjonctif stem: prenn- + -ent.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je veux que tu {___} la situation.
(I want you to understand the situation.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I want you to understand the situation.
facts.explanation: comprennes — que tu form of comprendre. Same pattern as prendre: comprenn- stem + -es for tu.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il est nécessaire que nous {___} les règles.
(It's necessary that we understand the rules.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It's necessary that we understand the rules.
facts.explanation: comprenions — que nous form of comprendre. Subjonctif stem: compren- + -ions.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il faut qu'ils {___} les conséquences de leurs actes.
(They need to understand the consequences of their actions.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: They need to understand the consequences of their actions.
facts.explanation: comprennent — qu'ils form of comprendre. Subjonctif stem: comprenn- + -ent.

facts.quizQuestion:
C'est important que j'{___} à cuisiner.
(It's important that I learn to cook.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It's important that I learn to cook.
facts.explanation: apprenne — que j' form of apprendre. Same pattern as prendre: apprenn- stem + -e for je.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je souhaite qu'ils {___} le français rapidement.
(I hope they learn French quickly.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I hope they learn French quickly.
facts.explanation: apprennent — qu'ils form of apprendre. Subjonctif stem: apprenn- + -ent.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il faut que j'{___} mon tour.
(I need to wait for my turn.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I need to wait for my turn.
facts.explanation: attende — que j' form of attendre. Formation: nous-form (attendons) minus -ons → attend- + -e. Unlike prendre, attendre does not double the final consonant.

facts.quizQuestion:
Elle préfère que nous {___} ici.
(She prefers that we wait here.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She prefers that we wait here.
facts.explanation: attendions — que nous form of attendre. Subjonctif stem: attend- + -ions.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il est nécessaire qu'ils {___} la réponse.
(It's necessary that they wait for the answer.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It's necessary that they wait for the answer.
facts.explanation: attendent — qu'ils form of attendre. Subjonctif stem: attend- + -ent.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il faut que je {___} à l'heure pour l'entretien.
(I need to be on time for the interview.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I need to be on time for the interview.
facts.explanation: sois — je/tu form of être in the subjonctif. Highly irregular: no resemblance to indicatif forms. Required after il faut que.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je veux que tu {___} honnête avec moi.
(I want you to be honest with me.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I want you to be honest with me.
facts.explanation: sois — tu form of être in the subjonctif (same as je). Required after je veux que.

facts.quizQuestion:
Que ce {___} fait immédiatement.
(Let it be done at once.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Let it be done at once.
facts.explanation: soit — il/elle form of être in the subjonctif. Que + subjonctif expresses a command or wish.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il est important que nous {___} patients.
(It is important that we be patient.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It is important that we be patient.
facts.explanation: soyons — nous form of être in the subjonctif. Required after il est important que.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je voudrais que vous {___} prêts à midi.
(I would like you to be ready at noon.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I would like you to be ready at noon.
facts.explanation: soyez — vous form of être in the subjonctif. Required after je voudrais que.

facts.quizQuestion:
Bénis {___} les pauvres d'esprit.
(Blessed are the poor in spirit.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Blessed are the poor in spirit.
facts.explanation: soient — ils/elles form of être in the subjonctif. Used here in an inverted que-clause expressing a wish or blessing.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il faut que j'{___} confiance en moi.
(I need to have confidence in myself.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I need to have confidence in myself.
facts.explanation: aie — je/tu form of avoir in the subjonctif. Highly irregular: not derived from the indicatif stem.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je ne veux pas que tu {___} peur.
(I don't want you to be afraid.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I don't want you to be afraid.
facts.explanation: aies — tu form of avoir in the subjonctif. Required after je ne veux pas que.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il est dommage qu'il n'{___} pas le temps.
(It's a shame he doesn't have time.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It's a shame he doesn't have time.
facts.explanation: ait — il/elle form of avoir in the subjonctif. Required after il est dommage que (expressing regret).

facts.quizQuestion:
Il vaut mieux que nous {___} de la patience.
(It's better that we have patience.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It's better that we have patience.
facts.explanation: ayons — nous form of avoir in the subjonctif. Required after il vaut mieux que.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il est nécessaire que vous {___} votre passeport.
(It is necessary that you have your passport.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It is necessary that you have your passport.
facts.explanation: ayez — vous form of avoir in the subjonctif. Required after il est nécessaire que.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je doute qu'ils {___} raison.
(I doubt they are right.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I doubt they are right.
facts.explanation: aient — ils/elles form of avoir in the subjonctif. Required after je doute que (expressing doubt).

facts.quizQuestion:
Il faut que j'{___} dormir.
(I have to go to sleep.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I have to go to sleep.
facts.explanation: aille — je/tu form of aller in the subjonctif. Irregular stem aill- used for je, tu, il, ils forms.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je ne veux pas que tu {___} là-bas seul.
(I don't want you to go there alone.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I don't want you to go there alone.
facts.explanation: ailles — tu form of aller in the subjonctif. Same irregular stem as je: aill-.

facts.quizQuestion:
J'aimerais que vous y {___}.
(I'd like for you to go there.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I'd like for you to go there.
facts.explanation: alliez — vous form of aller in the subjonctif. Nous/vous forms use the regular stem all- (from indicatif nous allons).

facts.quizQuestion:
Il est important qu'ils {___} à l'hôpital.
(It is important that they go to the hospital.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It is important that they go to the hospital.
facts.explanation: aillent — ils/elles form of aller in the subjonctif. Uses the same irregular stem aill- as je, tu, il.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il faut que je {___} la vaisselle ce soir.
(I need to do the dishes tonight.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I need to do the dishes tonight.
facts.explanation: fasse — je/tu form of faire in the subjonctif. Irregular stem fass- used for all persons except ils/elles.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je souhaite qu'il {___} beau demain.
(I hope the weather will be nice tomorrow.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I hope the weather will be nice tomorrow.
facts.explanation: fasse — il form of faire in the subjonctif. Faire beau = to be nice weather (impersonal). Required after je souhaite que.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il vaut mieux que nous {___} attention.
(It's better that we pay attention.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It's better that we pay attention.
facts.explanation: fassions — nous form of faire in the subjonctif. Uses the irregular stem fass- throughout.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je ne crois pas qu'ils {___} une erreur.
(I don't think they are making a mistake.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I don't think they are making a mistake.
facts.explanation: fassent — ils/elles form of faire in the subjonctif. Required after je ne crois pas que (negated belief).

facts.quizQuestion:
Il faut que je {___} partir avant midi.
(I need to be able to leave before noon.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I need to be able to leave before noon.
facts.explanation: puisse — je/tu form of pouvoir in the subjonctif. Irregular stem puiss-.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je doute qu'il {___} finir à temps.
(I doubt he can finish in time.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I doubt he can finish in time.
facts.explanation: puisse — il/elle form of pouvoir in the subjonctif. Stem puiss- used for je, tu, il, ils forms.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il est nécessaire que nous {___} communiquer en français.
(It is necessary that we be able to communicate in French.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It is necessary that we be able to communicate in French.
facts.explanation: puissions — nous form of pouvoir in the subjonctif. Irregular stem puiss- extends to nous/vous forms.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je souhaite qu'ils {___} venir à la fête.
(I hope they can come to the party.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I hope they can come to the party.
facts.explanation: puissent — ils/elles form of pouvoir in the subjonctif. Required after je souhaite que (wish).

facts.quizQuestion:
Il est dommage que je ne {___} pas y aller.
(It's a shame I don't want to go.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It's a shame I don't want to go.
facts.explanation: veuille — je/tu form of vouloir in the subjonctif. Irregular stem veuill-.

facts.quizQuestion:
J'aimerais qu'il {___} bien nous aider.
(I'd like him to be willing to help us.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I'd like him to be willing to help us.
facts.explanation: veuille — il/elle form of vouloir in the subjonctif. Vouloir bien = to be willing to. Required after j'aimerais que.

facts.quizQuestion:
Bien que nous {___} rester, il faut partir.
(Although we want to stay, we must leave.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Although we want to stay, we must leave.
facts.explanation: voulions — nous form of vouloir in the subjonctif. Bien que always requires subjonctif. Nous/vous forms use the regular stem voul-.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je ne pense pas qu'ils {___} vraiment changer.
(I don't think they really want to change.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I don't think they really want to change.
facts.explanation: veuillent — ils/elles form of vouloir in the subjonctif. Required after je ne pense pas que (negated belief).

facts.quizQuestion:
Il est important que je {___} la réponse.
(It is important that I know the answer.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It is important that I know the answer.
facts.explanation: sache — je/tu form of savoir in the subjonctif. Irregular stem sach-.

facts.quizQuestion:
Pas autant que je {___}.
(Not that I know of.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Not that I know of.
facts.explanation: sache — je form of savoir in the subjonctif. Autant que je sache is a fixed B1 expression meaning 'as far as I know'.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il faut que nous {___} utiliser ces outils.
(We need to know how to use these tools.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: We need to know how to use these tools.
facts.explanation: sachions — nous form of savoir in the subjonctif. Irregular stem sach- extends to nous/vous forms.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je veux qu'ils {___} la vérité.
(I want them to know the truth.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I want them to know the truth.
facts.explanation: sachent — ils/elles form of savoir in the subjonctif. Required after je veux que.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il ne faut pas qu'il {___} rater cette occasion.
(It must not happen that he misses this opportunity.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It must not happen that he misses this opportunity.
facts.explanation: faille — the sole form of falloir in the subjonctif (impersonal, il only). Irregular stem fail-.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je travaille sans qu'il {___} me le demander.
(I work without him having to ask me.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I work without him having to ask me.
facts.grammarPointLabel: falloir (il) — Subjonctif présent after sans que
facts.explanation: faille — il form of falloir in the subjonctif. Sans que always requires subjonctif. This pattern emphasises autonomous action.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je doute que ça {___} la peine.
(I doubt it's worth the trouble.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I doubt it's worth the trouble.
facts.explanation: vaille — il form of valoir in the subjonctif. Used in valoir la peine (to be worth the effort). Irregular stem vaill-.

facts.quizQuestion:
Bien que ce cours {___} beaucoup, c'est utile.
(Although this course costs a lot, it is useful.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Although this course costs a lot, it is useful.
facts.grammarPointLabel: valoir (il) — Subjonctif présent after bien que
facts.explanation: vaille — il form of valoir in the subjonctif. Bien que always requires subjonctif. Valoir here = to cost/be worth.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} tu finisses tes devoirs avant le dîner.
(You need to finish your homework before dinner.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: You need to finish your homework before dinner.
facts.grammarPointLabel: il faut que — necessity trigger → subjonctif
facts.explanation: il faut que is an impersonal necessity trigger that always requires the subjonctif. Here: il faut que tu finisses (finir, subjonctif).

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} vous soyez à l'heure demain matin.
(You must be on time tomorrow morning.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: You must be on time tomorrow morning.
facts.grammarPointLabel: il faut que — necessity trigger → subjonctif
facts.explanation: il faut que expresses impersonal necessity and requires the subjonctif. Here: il faut que vous soyez (être, subjonctif).

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} chacun comprenne les règles du jeu.
(It is necessary that everyone understand the rules of the game.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It is necessary that everyone understand the rules of the game.
facts.grammarPointLabel: il est nécessaire que — necessity trigger → subjonctif
facts.explanation: il est nécessaire que is a formal necessity expression that triggers the subjonctif. Here: il est nécessaire que chacun comprenne (comprendre, subjonctif).

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} nous ayons un passeport valide.
(It is necessary that we have a valid passport.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It is necessary that we have a valid passport.
facts.grammarPointLabel: il est nécessaire que — necessity trigger → subjonctif
facts.explanation: il est nécessaire que triggers the subjonctif. Here: il est nécessaire que nous ayons (avoir, subjonctif).

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} tu manges bien pendant ta maladie.
(It is important that you eat well during your illness.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It is important that you eat well during your illness.
facts.grammarPointLabel: il est important que — necessity trigger → subjonctif
facts.explanation: il est important que signals importance and requires the subjonctif. Here: il est important que tu manges (manger, subjonctif).

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} vous fassiez des exercices chaque jour.
(It is important that you exercise every day.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It is important that you exercise every day.
facts.grammarPointLabel: il est important que — necessity trigger → subjonctif
facts.explanation: il est important que requires the subjonctif. Here: il est important que vous fassiez (faire, subjonctif).

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} tu parles directement avec le directeur.
(It is better that you speak directly with the director.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It is better that you speak directly with the director.
facts.grammarPointLabel: il vaut mieux que — preference/necessity trigger → subjonctif
facts.explanation: il vaut mieux que (it is better that) is an impersonal expression requiring the subjonctif. Here: il vaut mieux que tu parles (parler, subjonctif).

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} nous restions ici et attendions.
(It is better that we stay here and wait.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It is better that we stay here and wait.
facts.grammarPointLabel: il vaut mieux que — preference/necessity trigger → subjonctif
facts.explanation: il vaut mieux que requires the subjonctif. Here: il vaut mieux que nous restions (rester, subjonctif).

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} tout le monde soit informé avant la réunion.
(It is essential that everyone be informed before the meeting.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It is essential that everyone be informed before the meeting.
facts.grammarPointLabel: il est indispensable que — strong necessity trigger → subjonctif
facts.explanation: il est indispensable que (it is essential/indispensable that) is a formal necessity trigger requiring the subjonctif. Here: il est indispensable que tout le monde soit (être, subjonctif).

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} tu viennes à ma fête d'anniversaire.
(I want you to come to my birthday party.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I want you to come to my birthday party.
facts.grammarPointLabel: je veux que — desire trigger → subjonctif
facts.explanation: je veux que (I want that) expresses desire and requires the subjonctif. Here: je veux que tu viennes (venir, subjonctif).

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} vous partiez maintenant.
(I want you to go now.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I want you to go now.
facts.grammarPointLabel: je veux que — desire trigger → subjonctif
facts.explanation: je veux que expresses desire and requires the subjonctif. Here: je veux que vous partiez (partir, subjonctif).

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} tu fasses moins de bruit.
(I would like you to make less noise.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I would like you to make less noise.
facts.grammarPointLabel: je voudrais que — polite desire trigger → subjonctif
facts.explanation: je voudrais que (I would like) is the conditional of vouloir used to express desire politely. It requires the subjonctif. Here: je voudrais que tu fasses (faire, subjonctif).

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} vous puissiez rester encore un peu.
(I would like you to be able to stay a little longer.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I would like you to be able to stay a little longer.
