Si vous m'écoutiez, vous {___} mieux la situation.
(If you listened to me, you would understand the situation better.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: If you listened to me, you would understand the situation better.
facts.explanation: comprendriez — conditionnel présent of comprendre (to understand), vous form. Irregular stem: comprendre → comprendr- + -iez.

facts.quizQuestion:
Si on travaillait ensemble, ce {___} plus facile pour tout le monde.
(If we worked together, it would be easier for everyone.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: If we worked together, it would be easier for everyone.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Si + imparfait → conditionnel présent — être (ce serait)
facts.explanation: serait — conditionnel présent of être (to be), il/elle/ce form. Irregular stem ser- + -ait. Ce serait is a common evaluative result clause in si-hypotheses.

facts.quizQuestion:
J'{___} être un oiseau pour voler librement.
(I wish I were a bird to fly freely.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I wish I were a bird to fly freely.
facts.explanation: aurais aimé — conditionnel passé of aimer. J'aurais aimé + être expresses a timeless wish for a different state of being. A common idiomatic use of conditionnel passé for unrealised desires.

facts.quizQuestion:
Si j'{___} le temps, je viendrais te voir.
(If I had time, I would come see you.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: If I had time, I would come see you.
facts.explanation: avais — imparfait of avoir in si-clause. Pattern: si + imparfait → conditionnel présent.

facts.quizQuestion:
Si tu {___} riche, que ferais-tu ?
(If you were rich, what would you do?)
facts.sentenceTranslation: If you were rich, what would you do?
facts.explanation: étais — imparfait of être in si-clause. Result clause uses conditionnel présent.

facts.quizQuestion:
Si je {___} voler, je serais oiseau.
(If I could fly, I would be a bird.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: If I could fly, I would be a bird.
facts.explanation: pouvais — imparfait of pouvoir in si-clause. Si + imparfait → conditionnel présent.

facts.quizQuestion:
J'{___} ma montre avant de partir.
(I had repaired my watch before leaving.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I had repaired my watch before leaving.
facts.explanation: avais réparé — j'avais (imparfait of avoir) + réparé (past participle of réparer). PQP marks an action completed before another past action.

facts.quizQuestion:
Maman ne l'{___} pas avant ce jour.
(Mom hadn't mentioned it before that day.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Mom hadn't mentioned it before that day.
facts.explanation: avait mentionné — elle avait (imparfait of avoir) + mentionné (past participle of mentionner). Negation: n'avait pas mentionné.

facts.quizQuestion:
J'{___} à mon professeur avant l'examen.
(I had spoken to my teacher before the exam.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I had spoken to my teacher before the exam.
facts.explanation: avais parlé — j'avais (imparfait of avoir) + parlé (past participle of parler). PQP = auxiliary at imparfait + past participle.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il {___} ses devoirs quand je suis arrivé.
(He had finished his homework when I arrived.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He had finished his homework when I arrived.
facts.explanation: avait fini — il avait (imparfait of avoir) + fini (past participle of finir). Regular -ir verb past participle ends in -i.

facts.quizQuestion:
Nous {___} le train avant que la grève commence.
(We had taken the train before the strike started.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: We had taken the train before the strike started.
facts.explanation: avions pris — nous avions (imparfait of avoir) + pris (irregular past participle of prendre).

facts.quizQuestion:
Vous {___} la vérité, mais personne ne vous avait cru.
(You had told the truth, but nobody had believed you.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: You had told the truth, but nobody had believed you.
facts.explanation: aviez dit — vous aviez (imparfait of avoir) + dit (irregular past participle of dire).

facts.quizQuestion:
Ils {___} leurs valises la veille du départ.
(They had packed their bags the day before leaving.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: They had packed their bags the day before leaving.
facts.explanation: avaient fait — ils avaient (imparfait of avoir) + fait (irregular past participle of faire).

facts.quizQuestion:
Tu {___} ce film avant moi, donc tu savais la fin.
(You had seen that film before me, so you knew the ending.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: You had seen that film before me, so you knew the ending.
facts.explanation: avais vu — tu avais (imparfait of avoir) + vu (irregular past participle of voir).

facts.quizQuestion:
Nous {___} avant d'aller au cinéma.
(We had eaten before going to the cinema.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: We had eaten before going to the cinema.
facts.explanation: avions mangé — nous avions (imparfait of avoir) + mangé (past participle of manger). Regular -er verb.

facts.quizQuestion:
Elle {___} la lettre avant que je rentre.
(She had written the letter before I got home.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She had written the letter before I got home.
facts.explanation: avait écrit — elle avait (imparfait of avoir) + écrit (irregular past participle of écrire).

facts.quizQuestion:
Vous {___} le rapport avant la réunion.
(You had read the report before the meeting.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: You had read the report before the meeting.
facts.explanation: aviez lu — vous aviez (imparfait of avoir) + lu (irregular past participle of lire).

facts.quizQuestion:
Ils {___} la leçon avant l'examen.
(They had understood the lesson before the exam.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: They had understood the lesson before the exam.
facts.explanation: avaient compris — ils avaient (imparfait of avoir) + compris (irregular past participle of comprendre).

facts.quizQuestion:
Tu {___} longtemps avant de partir.
(You had waited a long time before leaving.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: You had waited a long time before leaving.
facts.explanation: avais attendu — tu avais (imparfait of avoir) + attendu (past participle of attendre). Regular -re verb.

facts.quizQuestion:
J'{___} en France avant d'apprendre le français.
(I had gone to France before learning French.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I had gone to France before learning French.
facts.explanation: étais allé(e) — j'étais (imparfait of être) + allé(e) (past participle of aller). Être verbs require past participle agreement with subject.

facts.quizQuestion:
Elle {___} quand j'ai appelé.
(She had left when I called.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She had left when I called.
facts.explanation: était partie — elle était (imparfait of être) + partie (past participle of partir, feminine agreement: parti + e).

facts.quizQuestion:
Nous {___} avant la pluie.
(We had gone out before the rain.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: We had gone out before the rain.
facts.explanation: étions sortis — nous étions (imparfait of être) + sortis (past participle of sortir, masculine plural agreement).

facts.quizQuestion:
Vous {___} ici l'année précédente.
(You had come here the previous year.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: You had come here the previous year.
facts.explanation: étiez venu(e)(s) — vous étiez (imparfait of être) + venu(e)(s) (past participle of venir, agreement with vous referent).

facts.quizQuestion:
Les feuilles {___} avant l'hiver.
(The leaves had fallen before winter.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The leaves had fallen before winter.
facts.explanation: étaient tombées — elles étaient (imparfait of être) + tombées (past participle of tomber, feminine plural agreement: tombé + es).

facts.quizQuestion:
Il {___} avant tout le monde.
(He had arrived before everyone else.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He had arrived before everyone else.
facts.explanation: était arrivé — il était (imparfait of être) + arrivé (past participle of arriver, masculine singular: no agreement suffix added).

facts.quizQuestion:
Elle {___} dans cette ville avant que ses parents déménagent.
(She had been born in that city before her parents moved.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She had been born in that city before her parents moved.
facts.explanation: était née — elle était (imparfait of être) + née (past participle of naître, feminine agreement: né + e).

facts.quizQuestion:
Ils {___} longtemps à Paris avant de rentrer.
(They had stayed in Paris a long time before going home.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: They had stayed in Paris a long time before going home.
facts.explanation: étaient restés — ils étaient (imparfait of être) + restés (past participle of rester, masculine plural agreement).

facts.quizQuestion:
Il {___} quand le téléphone a sonné.
(He had gone out when the phone rang.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He had gone out when the phone rang.
facts.explanation: était sorti — il était (imparfait of être) + sorti (past participle of sortir, masculine singular).

facts.quizQuestion:
Je {___} tôt ce jour-là.
(I had gotten up early that day.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I had gotten up early that day.
facts.explanation: m'étais levé(e) — me + j'étais (imparfait of être) + levé(e) (past participle of lever, agreement with subject). Reflexive verbs always use être.

facts.quizQuestion:
Elle {___} avant minuit.
(She had gone to bed before midnight.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She had gone to bed before midnight.
facts.explanation: s'était couchée — se + elle était (imparfait of être) + couchée (past participle, feminine agreement: couché + e). Reflexive PQP requires être.

facts.quizQuestion:
Ils {___} avant la fin du film.
(They had fallen asleep before the end of the film.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: They had fallen asleep before the end of the film.
facts.explanation: s'étaient endormis — se + ils étaient (imparfait of être) + endormis (past participle of endormir, masculine plural agreement).

facts.quizQuestion:
Tu {___} quand j'ai frappé à la porte.
(You had gotten up when I knocked on the door.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: You had gotten up when I knocked on the door.
facts.explanation: t'étais levé(e) — te + tu étais (imparfait of être) + levé(e) (past participle of lever, agreement with tu referent).

facts.quizQuestion:
Il {___} quand nous sommes arrivés au restaurant.
(He had already eaten when we arrived at the restaurant.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He had already eaten when we arrived at the restaurant.
facts.explanation: avait déjà mangé — il avait (imparfait of avoir) + déjà + mangé. Déjà (already) typically sits between the auxiliary and the past participle in PQP.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je {___} Paris avant ce voyage.
(I had never visited Paris before that trip.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I had never visited Paris before that trip.
facts.explanation: n'avais jamais visité — ne + j'avais + jamais + visité. In PQP negation, ne precedes the auxiliary and jamais follows it.

facts.quizQuestion:
Quand j'ai appelé, il {___} depuis une heure.
(When I called, he had been home for an hour.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: When I called, he had been home for an hour.
facts.grammarPointLabel: rentrer (il) avec quand — PQP séquence temporelle
facts.explanation: était rentré — il était (imparfait of être) + rentré (past participle of rentrer). Quand + PQP signals an action already completed at the time of another past action.

facts.quizQuestion:
Elle {___} ses clés, donc elle ne pouvait pas entrer.
(She had forgotten her keys, so she couldn't get in.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She had forgotten her keys, so she couldn't get in.
facts.explanation: avait oublié — elle avait (imparfait of avoir) + oublié (past participle of oublier). PQP explains why a past situation existed.

facts.quizQuestion:
C'est le livre {___} je te parlais hier.
(That's the book I was telling you about yesterday.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: That's the book I was telling you about yesterday.
facts.explanation: dont replaces de + noun after verbs taking de. Parler de → dont je te parlais.

facts.quizQuestion:
Voici le médecin {___} ma mère a besoin.
(Here is the doctor my mother needs.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Here is the doctor my mother needs.
facts.grammarPointLabel: dont — replaces de + noun (avoir besoin de)
facts.explanation: dont replaces de + noun after avoir besoin de. Avoir besoin de → dont elle a besoin.

facts.quizQuestion:
C'est un sujet {___} nous avons souvent discuté.
(It's a subject we have often discussed.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It's a subject we have often discussed.
facts.explanation: dont replaces de + noun after discuter de. Discuter de → dont nous avons discuté.

facts.quizQuestion:
C'est l'étudiant {___} le père est professeur.
(He is the student whose father is a teacher.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He is the student whose father is a teacher.
facts.explanation: dont expresses possession: C'est l'étudiant dont le père est professeur = the student whose father is a teacher. The possessed noun follows directly.

facts.quizQuestion:
La maison {___} les fenêtres sont vertes est ancienne.
(The house whose windows are green is old.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The house whose windows are green is old.
facts.grammarPointLabel: dont — possession (les fenêtres de la maison)
facts.explanation: dont used for possession with a non-human antecedent. Les fenêtres de la maison → dont les fenêtres sont vertes.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il a réussi l'examen {___} il était si fier.
(He passed the exam he was so proud of.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He passed the exam he was so proud of.
facts.grammarPointLabel: dont — replaces de + noun (être fier de)
facts.explanation: dont replaces de + noun after adjectives taking de. Être fier de → dont il était si fier.

facts.quizQuestion:
La ville {___} je vis est très animée.
(The city where I live is very lively.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The city where I live is very lively.
facts.explanation: où replaces a location expression. La ville → où je vis. It is equivalent to dans laquelle here.

facts.quizQuestion:
C'est le restaurant {___} nous nous sommes rencontrés.
(That's the restaurant where we met.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: That's the restaurant where we met.
facts.explanation: où replaces a location. Le restaurant → où nous nous sommes rencontrés.

facts.quizQuestion:
Le jour {___} il est né, il neigeait.
(The day he was born, it was snowing.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The day he was born, it was snowing.
facts.explanation: où can replace a time expression. Le jour → où il est né. This temporal use is characteristic of B1.

facts.quizQuestion:
À l'époque {___} j'étudiais, internet n'existait pas.
(In the era when I was studying, the internet didn't exist.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: In the era when I was studying, the internet didn't exist.
facts.explanation: où replaces the time expression l'époque. À l'époque où is a fixed B1 pattern for referring to a past period.

facts.quizQuestion:
Voilà le bureau {___} je travaille chaque matin.
(There is the office where I work every morning.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: There is the office where I work every morning.
facts.explanation: où replaces a location. Le bureau → où je travaille.

facts.quizQuestion:
Dis-moi {___} est arrivé.
(Tell me what happened.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Tell me what happened.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ce qui — subject of indefinite antecedent
facts.explanation: ce qui is the subject of the relative clause with an indefinite antecedent. Ce qui est arrivé = what happened (subject role).

facts.quizQuestion:
Qu'est-ce {___} t'a réveillé ?
(What woke you up?)
facts.sentenceTranslation: What woke you up?
facts.explanation: ce qui is the subject. In qu'est-ce qui, qui is the subject pronoun asking about the subject of the verb.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je ne comprends pas {___} se passe ici.
(I don't understand what is happening here.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I don't understand what is happening here.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ce qui — subject of indefinite antecedent
facts.explanation: ce qui is the subject of se passe. The antecedent is indefinite — no specific noun precedes it.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} m'intéresse, c'est l'histoire.
(What interests me is history.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: What interests me is history.
facts.explanation: ce qui is the subject of m'intéresse. It appears at the start of a cleft sentence to emphasize the topic.

facts.quizQuestion:
C'est {___} je pensais.
(That's what I thought.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: That's what I thought.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ce que — direct object of indefinite antecedent
facts.explanation: ce que is the direct object of pensais. Ce que je pensais = what I thought (object role).

facts.quizQuestion:
Mangez {___} vous voulez.
(Eat whatever you like.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Eat whatever you like.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ce que — direct object, indefinite antecedent
facts.explanation: ce que is the direct object of voulez. Ce que vous voulez = what(ever) you want.

facts.quizQuestion:
Fais {___} tu aimes.
(Do what you like.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Do what you like.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ce que — direct object, indefinite antecedent
facts.explanation: ce que is the direct object of aimes. Ce que tu aimes = what you like.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je ne sais pas {___} elle veut dire.
(I don't know what she means.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I don't know what she means.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ce que — direct object, indefinite antecedent
facts.explanation: ce que is the direct object of veut dire. Ce que elle veut dire = what she means.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je t'ai donné {___} tu avais besoin.
(I gave you what you needed.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I gave you what you needed.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ce dont — indefinite antecedent + avoir besoin de
facts.explanation: ce dont replaces de + indefinite antecedent. Avoir besoin de → ce dont tu avais besoin = what you needed.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} j'ai peur, c'est de l'échec.
(What I'm afraid of is failure.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: What I'm afraid of is failure.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ce dont — indefinite antecedent + avoir peur de
facts.explanation: ce dont replaces de + indefinite antecedent. Avoir peur de → ce dont j'ai peur = what I'm afraid of.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il m'a expliqué {___} il parlait.
(He explained to me what he was talking about.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He explained to me what he was talking about.
facts.grammarPointLabel: ce dont — indefinite antecedent + parler de
facts.explanation: ce dont replaces de + indefinite antecedent. Parler de → ce dont il parlait = what he was talking about.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} hésite est perdu.
(He who hesitates is lost.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He who hesitates is lost.
facts.explanation: celui qui is the masculine singular demonstrative relative pronoun acting as subject. Refers to an unspecified male person.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je préfère la robe bleue, pas {___} est rouge.
(I prefer the blue dress, not the one that is red.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I prefer the blue dress, not the one that is red.
facts.explanation: celle qui is feminine singular (robe is feminine). It replaces la robe + qui and acts as the subject of est.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je voudrais ce gâteau, pas {___} tu as choisi.
(I'd like this cake, not the one you chose.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I'd like this cake, not the one you chose.
facts.explanation: celui que is masculine singular (le gâteau). It replaces le gâteau + que and acts as the direct object of as choisi.

facts.quizQuestion:
Mangez celle-ci, pas {___} je t'ai montrée.
(Eat this one, not the one I showed you.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Eat this one, not the one I showed you.
facts.explanation: celle que is feminine singular (implied feminine noun). It replaces la chose + que and acts as direct object of montrée.

facts.quizQuestion:
J'aime tous mes étudiants, surtout {___} travaillent dur.
(I love all my students, especially those who work hard.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I love all my students, especially those who work hard.
facts.explanation: ceux qui is masculine plural (les étudiants, masculine plural). Acts as subject of travaillent.

facts.quizQuestion:
Parmi ces livres, ce sont {___} j'ai lus l'été dernier.
(Among these books, those are the ones I read last summer.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Among these books, those are the ones I read last summer.
facts.explanation: ceux que is masculine plural (les livres). Acts as direct object of ai lus — note past participle agreement with masculine plural.

facts.quizQuestion:
Le problème, c'est {___} je t'ai parlé hier.
(The problem is the one I told you about yesterday.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The problem is the one I told you about yesterday.
facts.grammarPointLabel: celui dont — masculine singular + de complement
facts.explanation: celui dont combines the demonstrative celui with dont (replacing de). Parler de → celui dont je t'ai parlé = the one I talked about.

facts.quizQuestion:
Mon appartement préféré était {___} j'habitais à Lyon.
(My favourite apartment was the one where I lived in Lyon.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: My favourite apartment was the one where I lived in Lyon.
facts.explanation: celui où combines the demonstrative celui with où. Refers back to an apartment (place antecedent): celui où j'habitais = the one where I lived.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il {___} donne chaque matin.
(He gives it to me every morning.)
Choose the correct double pronoun pair.
facts.sentenceTranslation: He gives it to me every morning.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Double pronoun: me + le — order rule
facts.explanation: me le — indirect object me comes before direct object le. Rule: me/te/nous/vous precede le/la/les. × le me is wrong.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je {___} donnerai demain.
(I will give it to you tomorrow.)
Choose the correct double pronoun pair.
facts.sentenceTranslation: I will give it to you tomorrow.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Double pronoun: te + le — order rule
facts.explanation: te le — te (indirect, you) precedes le (direct, it). Based on Tatoeba #12413: Je te le donnerai.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je {___} prêterai si tu veux.
(I'll lend it to you if you want.)
Choose the correct double pronoun pair.
facts.sentenceTranslation: I'll lend it to you if you want.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Double pronoun: te + la — order rule
facts.explanation: te la — te (indirect, to you) precedes la (direct object, feminine). Based on Tatoeba #492320: Je te la prêterai.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je {___} laisse — prends-le !
(I'm leaving it to you — take it!)
Choose the correct double pronoun pair.
facts.sentenceTranslation: I'm leaving it to you — take it!
facts.explanation: te le — te (to you, indirect) precedes le (it, direct). Based on Tatoeba #477142: Je te le laisse.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je voulais {___} montrer hier.
(I wanted to show it to you yesterday.)
Choose the correct double pronoun pair.
facts.sentenceTranslation: I wanted to show it to you yesterday.
facts.explanation: te le — te (to you) comes before le (it). Based on Tatoeba #563219: Je voulais te le montrer.

facts.quizQuestion:
Elle {___} explique chaque semaine.
(She explains them to us every week.)
Choose the correct double pronoun pair.
facts.sentenceTranslation: She explains them to us every week.
facts.explanation: nous les — nous (to us, indirect) precedes les (them, direct plural). Rule: me/te/nous/vous always come before le/la/les.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il {___} montrera à la réunion.
(He will show it to you at the meeting.)
Choose the correct double pronoun pair.
facts.sentenceTranslation: He will show it to you at the meeting.
facts.explanation: vous la — vous (to you, indirect) precedes la (it, feminine direct). The indirect pronoun groupe me/te/nous/vous always comes first.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je {___} donne chaque soir.
(I give it to him every evening.)
Choose the correct double pronoun pair.
facts.sentenceTranslation: I give it to him every evening.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Double pronoun: le + lui — order rule
facts.explanation: le lui — le (direct object) comes before lui (indirect). Critical rule: lui/leur always follow le/la/les. × lui le is a common error.

facts.quizQuestion:
Oses-tu {___} demander ?
(Dare you ask him about it?)
Choose the correct double pronoun pair.
facts.sentenceTranslation: Dare you ask him about it?
facts.explanation: le lui — le (it, direct) before lui (him, indirect). Based on Tatoeba #4926556: Oses-tu le lui demander ?

facts.quizQuestion:
Elle {___} montre avec plaisir.
(She shows it to them gladly.)
Choose the correct double pronoun pair.
facts.sentenceTranslation: She shows it to them gladly.
facts.explanation: la leur — la (it, feminine direct) before leur (to them, indirect). leur comes after le/la/les, never before.

facts.quizQuestion:
Nous {___} envoyons chaque mois.
(We send them to them every month.)
Choose the correct double pronoun pair.
facts.sentenceTranslation: We send them to them every month.
facts.explanation: les leur — les (them, direct plural) before leur (to them, indirect). × leur les is the incorrect order.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il me l'a dit — mais je ne {___} ai pas encore dit.
(He told me — but I haven't told him yet.)
Choose the correct double pronoun pair for the blank.
facts.sentenceTranslation: He told me — but I haven't told him yet.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Double pronoun: la + lui — passé composé
facts.explanation: la lui — la (it, feminine) before lui (to him). In passé composé double pronouns: ne + la + lui + ai + pas + dit.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il {___} a parlé hier soir.
(He spoke to me about it last night.)
Choose the correct double pronoun.
facts.sentenceTranslation: He spoke to me about it last night.
facts.explanation: m'en — me becomes m' before en. Verb parler de → en replaces de + noun; me = indirect (to me). Order: me/te/nous/vous before en.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je {___} parlerai demain, promis.
(I'll talk to you about it tomorrow, I promise.)
Choose the correct double pronoun.
facts.sentenceTranslation: I'll talk to you about it tomorrow, I promise.
facts.explanation: t'en — te becomes t' before en. parler de → en replaces de + noun; te = indirect (to you). me/te come before en.

facts.quizQuestion:
Elle {___} a donné trois.
(She gave us three of them.)
Choose the correct double pronoun.
facts.sentenceTranslation: She gave us three of them.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Double pronoun: nous en — quantity + en
facts.explanation: nous en — nous (to us, indirect) before en (of them). With quantity expressions, en stays and the number follows the verb.

facts.quizQuestion:
Complete the sentence: {___} beaucoup dans ce magasin.
(There are many of them in this shop.)
Choose the correct double pronoun phrase.
facts.sentenceTranslation: There are many of them in this shop.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Double pronoun: il y en a — there is/are some
facts.explanation: il y en a — the fixed phrase meaning 'there is/are some'. y (location/thing) comes before en. This y+en combination is the most common.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je {___} souviens très bien.
(I remember it very well.)
Choose the correct double pronoun.
facts.sentenceTranslation: I remember it very well.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Double pronoun: m'en — se souvenir de
facts.explanation: m'en — se souvenir de → en replaces de + thing; me becomes m' before en. Je me souviens de cela → Je m'en souviens.

facts.quizQuestion:
Give it to him! — Which affirmative imperative form is correct?
(le = the book, lui = to him)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Give it to him!
facts.explanation: Donne-le-lui ! — In affirmative imperatives, pronouns follow the verb with hyphens: verb-DO-IO. le (direct) before lui (indirect). × Donne-lui-le is wrong.

facts.quizQuestion:
Show it to them! — Which affirmative imperative form is correct?
(la = the photo, leur = to them)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Show it to them!
facts.explanation: Montre-la-leur ! — Affirmative imperative order: verb-DO-IO. la (direct, feminine) before leur (indirect, to them). × Montre-leur-la is wrong.

facts.quizQuestion:
Lend it to me! — Which affirmative imperative form is correct?
(le = the pen, moi = to me)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Lend it to me!
facts.grammarPointLabel: Imperative double pronoun: Prête-le-moi — stressed form
facts.explanation: Prête-le-moi ! — In affirmative imperatives, me becomes stressed moi after the verb. Order: verb-le-moi (DO before IO). Based on arch example.

facts.quizQuestion:
Don't tell it to me. — Which negative imperative form is correct?
(le = it, me = to me)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Don't tell it to me.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Negative imperative double pronoun: Ne me le dis pas
facts.explanation: Ne me le dis pas. — In negative imperatives, pronouns stay before the verb in normal declarative order: ne + me + le + verb + pas. me stays as me (not moi).

facts.quizQuestion:
Don't give it to him. — Which negative imperative form is correct?
(le = it, lui = to him)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Don't give it to him.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Negative imperative double pronoun: Ne le lui donne pas
facts.explanation: Ne le lui donne pas. — Negative imperative keeps declarative pronoun order before verb: ne + le + lui + donne + pas. × Ne lui le donne pas is the error form.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il {___} a dit hier. (passé composé)
(He told it to me yesterday.)
Choose the correct double pronoun pair.
facts.sentenceTranslation: He told it to me yesterday.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Double pronoun: me + le — passé composé
facts.explanation: me le — in passé composé, double pronouns still precede avoir/être: Il me le dit → Il me l'a dit (when le contracts before vowel). Here no contraction: me le + a dit.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je vous {___} ai déjà dit.
(I already told it to you.)
Choose the correct double pronoun pair for lui/leur slot.
(Replace: je l'ai dit à lui)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I already told it to him.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Double pronoun: le + lui — déjà dit (passé composé)
facts.explanation: le lui — Based on Tatoeba #11889: Je vous l'ai déjà dit. Here lui replaces à lui (indirect). le (direct) always precedes lui.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} juste un peu.
(Give me just a little of it.)
Choose the correct imperative form with double pronoun.
facts.sentenceTranslation: Give me just a little of it.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Imperative double pronoun: Donnez-m'en — stressed moi+en
facts.explanation: Donnez-m'en — In affirmative imperative, moi + en contracts to m'en with elision. Tatoeba #15088: Donnez-m'en juste un peu.

facts.quizQuestion:
Ferme la porte {___}.
(Close the door when you leave.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Close the door when you leave.
facts.explanation: en sortant — gérondif of sortir. Formed with en + present participle: sort- + -ant. Expresses simultaneity: 'while leaving'.

facts.quizQuestion:
J'ai lu un livre {___}.
(I read a book while eating.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I read a book while eating.
facts.explanation: en mangeant — gérondif of manger. Stem mang- + -eant (spelling adjustment: mange → mangeant keeps the soft-g). Expresses two simultaneous actions.

facts.quizQuestion:
Affronte la vie {___} !
(Face life with a smile!)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Face life with a smile!
facts.explanation: en souriant — gérondif of sourire. Stem souri- + -ant. Expresses manner: 'by smiling', 'with a smile'.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il a appris l'anglais {___} avec des natifs.
(He learned English by speaking with native speakers.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He learned English by speaking with native speakers.
facts.explanation: en parlant — gérondif of parler. Regular -er: stem parl- + -ant, preceded by en. Expresses the means by which something is achieved.

facts.quizQuestion:
Elle fait ses devoirs {___} de la musique.
(She does her homework while listening to music.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She does her homework while listening to music.
facts.explanation: en écoutant — gérondif of écouter. Stem écout- + -ant. The en + participle construction marks two simultaneous actions.

facts.quizQuestion:
On réussit {___} dur.
(One succeeds by working hard.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: One succeeds by working hard.
facts.explanation: en travaillant — gérondif of travailler. Stem travaill- + -ant. Expresses the means: 'by working hard'.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il est arrivé à l'heure {___}.
(He arrived on time by running.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He arrived on time by running.
facts.explanation: en courant — gérondif of courir. Irregular present participle stem: cour- + -ant. Expresses how he arrived.

facts.quizQuestion:
J'ai passé la soirée {___} sur le canapé.
(I spent the evening reading on the sofa.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I spent the evening reading on the sofa.
facts.explanation: en lisant — gérondif of lire. Present participle stem: lis- + -ant. The gérondif here describes what was done during the evening.

facts.quizQuestion:
Elle téléphone {___} dans la rue.
(She talks on the phone while walking in the street.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She talks on the phone while walking in the street.
facts.explanation: en marchant — gérondif of marcher. Regular -er: stem march- + -ant. Describes simultaneous action: walking and talking on the phone.

facts.quizQuestion:
Tu gagneras du temps {___} plus tôt.
(You will save time by finishing earlier.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: You will save time by finishing earlier.
facts.explanation: en finissant — gérondif of finir. Regular -ir: stem finiss- + -ant. The -iss- infix appears in the present participle of regular -ir verbs.

facts.quizQuestion:
Tu éviteras les embouteillages {___} le métro.
(You will avoid traffic jams by taking the metro.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: You will avoid traffic jams by taking the metro.
facts.explanation: en prenant — gérondif of prendre. Irregular present participle: pren- + -ant. The gérondif expresses the means of avoiding traffic.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je l'ai rencontré {___} au marché.
(I met him while going to the market.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I met him while going to the market.
facts.explanation: en allant — gérondif of aller. Irregular stem: all- + -ant. Although aller is highly irregular in conjugation, its present participle stem is all-.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} patient, on peut tout apprendre.
(By being patient, one can learn anything.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: By being patient, one can learn anything.
facts.explanation: en étant — gérondif of être. Irregular: the present participle of être is étant (not *esant). One of three fully irregular participe présent forms in French.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} de la chance, il a trouvé un appartement rapidement.
(By having luck, he found an apartment quickly.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: By having luck, he found an apartment quickly.
facts.explanation: en ayant — gérondif of avoir. Irregular: the present participle of avoir is ayant (not *avant). Used with en to form the gérondif.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} parler plusieurs langues, elle a trouvé un travail facilement.
(Knowing several languages, she found a job easily.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Knowing several languages, she found a job easily.
facts.explanation: en sachant — gérondif of savoir. Irregular: present participle is sachant (not *savant). One of three fully irregular participe présent forms.

facts.quizQuestion:
J'achète le pain {___} du bureau.
(I buy bread on my way home from the office.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I buy bread on my way home from the office.
facts.explanation: en rentrant — gérondif of rentrer. Stem rentr- + -ant. Describes an action done simultaneously with or during the act of going home.

facts.quizQuestion:
Elle a vu ses amis {___} à la gare.
(She saw her friends when arriving at the station.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She saw her friends when arriving at the station.
facts.explanation: en arrivant — gérondif of arriver. Stem arriv- + -ant. Used here to express the moment of arriving as a time reference.

facts.quizQuestion:
Tu amélioreras ton français {___} des exercices chaque jour.
(You will improve your French by doing exercises every day.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: You will improve your French by doing exercises every day.
facts.explanation: en faisant — gérondif of faire. Irregular present participle: faisant (not *faissat). The -ais- stem is shared with the imparfait.

facts.quizQuestion:
N'oublie pas d'éteindre les lumières {___}.
(Don't forget to turn off the lights when leaving.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Don't forget to turn off the lights when leaving.
facts.explanation: en partant — gérondif of partir. Stem part- + -ant. Similar to en sortant, used for actions done at the moment of departing.

facts.quizQuestion:
Elle fait la vaisselle {___}.
(She does the dishes while singing.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She does the dishes while singing.
facts.explanation: en chantant — gérondif of chanter. Regular -er: stem chant- + -ant. Describes a concurrent activity.

facts.quizQuestion:
C'est un fossile {___} !
(He is a living fossil!)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He is a living fossil!
facts.explanation: vivant — participe présent of vivre used as an adjective meaning 'living'. As an adjective it agrees in gender/number: vivant / vivante / vivants / vivantes.

facts.quizQuestion:
C'est une personne très {___}.
(She is a very cheerful / smiling person.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She is a very cheerful / smiling person.
facts.explanation: souriant — participe présent of sourire used adjectivally, meaning 'smiling, cheerful'. Agrees with the noun: souriante for feminine.

facts.quizQuestion:
C'est un enfant très {___}.
(He is a very obedient child.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He is a very obedient child.
facts.explanation: obéissant — participe présent of obéir used adjectivally. Regular -ir verb: obéiss- + -ant. As an adjective it means 'obedient'.

facts.quizQuestion:
Ils ont passé la soirée dans un restaurant {___}.
(They spent the evening in a charming restaurant.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: They spent the evening in a charming restaurant.
facts.explanation: charmant — participe présent of charmer, now fully lexicalized as an adjective meaning 'charming'. This is a common pattern: participes présents often become independent adjectives.

facts.quizQuestion:
Les étudiants {___} dans la bibliothèque ont besoin de silence.
(The students working in the library need silence.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The students working in the library need silence.
facts.explanation: travaillant — participe présent of travailler used verbally to modify a noun (like a relative clause). In this verbal use it is invariable: no agreement with the noun.

facts.quizQuestion:
Veuillez lire le chapitre {___}.
(Please read the following chapter.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Please read the following chapter.
facts.explanation: suivant — participe présent of suivre, fully lexicalized as an adjective/determiner meaning 'following, next'. Agrees with noun: suivante for feminine.

facts.quizQuestion:
L'équipe {___} recevra un trophée.
(The winning team will receive a trophy.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The winning team will receive a trophy.
facts.explanation: gagnant — participe présent of gagner used adjectivally: 'winning'. As an adjective it agrees: gagnante (fem.), gagnants (masc. pl.), gagnantes (fem. pl.).

facts.quizQuestion:
Il ne faut pas être mauvais {___}.
(You shouldn't be a sore loser.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: You shouldn't be a sore loser.
facts.explanation: perdant — participe présent of perdre, nominalized to mean 'loser, the losing side'. A common pattern: the participe présent can function as a noun.

facts.quizQuestion:
C'est un étudiant {___}.
(He is a brilliant student.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He is a brilliant student.
facts.explanation: brillant — participe présent of briller, fully lexicalized as an adjective meaning 'brilliant, bright'. Agrees with noun: brillante for feminine.

facts.quizQuestion:
Ce professeur est très {___}.
(This teacher is very demanding.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: This teacher is very demanding.
facts.explanation: exigeant — participe présent of exiger, used adjectivally: 'demanding, exacting'. Note the spelling: exig- + -eant (soft-g preservation). Feminine: exigeante.

facts.quizQuestion:
La lettre {___} Marie chaque semaine.
(The letter is written by Marie every week.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The letter is written by Marie every week.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Passif présent — est + PP (féminin singulier) + par
facts.explanation: est écrite par — passif présent de écrire. Sujet féminin singulier (la lettre) → participe passé accordé : écrite.

facts.quizQuestion:
Ce roman {___} un grand éditeur parisien.
(This novel is published by a major Parisian publisher.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: This novel is published by a major Parisian publisher.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Passif présent — est + PP (masculin singulier) + par
facts.explanation: est publié par — passif présent de publier. Sujet masculin singulier (ce roman) → participe passé non accordé : publié.

facts.quizQuestion:
Ces réunions {___} le directeur tous les lundis.
(These meetings are organised by the director every Monday.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: These meetings are organised by the director every Monday.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Passif présent — sont + PP (féminin pluriel) + par
facts.explanation: sont organisées par — passif présent de organiser. Sujet féminin pluriel (ces réunions) → participe passé accordé : organisées.

facts.quizQuestion:
Mike {___} les membres du comité.
(Mike was elected by the members of the committee.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Mike was elected by the members of the committee.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Passif passé composé — a été + PP (masculin) + par
facts.explanation: a été élu par — passif au passé composé de élire. Sujet masculin singulier → participe passé : élu.

facts.quizQuestion:
La proposition {___} la commission.
(The proposal was rejected by the committee.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The proposal was rejected by the committee.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Passif passé composé — a été + PP (féminin) + par
facts.explanation: a été rejetée par — passif au passé composé de rejeter. Sujet féminin singulier (la proposition) → participe passé accordé : rejetée.

facts.quizQuestion:
Beaucoup d'arbres {___} la tempête.
(Many trees were knocked down by the storm.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Many trees were knocked down by the storm.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Passif passé composé — ont été + PP (masculin pluriel) + par
facts.explanation: ont été abattus par — passif au passé composé de abattre. Sujet masculin pluriel (beaucoup d'arbres) → participe passé accordé : abattus.

facts.quizQuestion:
Le parlement {___} le président.
(Parliament was dissolved by the president.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Parliament was dissolved by the president.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Passif passé composé — a été + PP (masculin irrégulier) + par
facts.explanation: a été dissous par — passif au passé composé de dissoudre. Participe passé irrégulier : dissous (masculin singulier).

facts.quizQuestion:
La cathédrale {___} des ouvriers depuis des siècles.
(The cathedral was being built by workers for centuries.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The cathedral was being built by workers for centuries.
facts.explanation: était construite par — passif à l'imparfait de construire. L'imparfait du passif exprime une action continue dans le passé. Sujet féminin (la cathédrale) → construite.

facts.quizQuestion:
Ce bâtiment {___} les soldats pendant la guerre.
(This building was used by soldiers during the war.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: This building was used by soldiers during the war.
facts.explanation: était utilisé par — passif à l'imparfait de utiliser. Sujet masculin singulier (ce bâtiment) → participe passé non accordé : utilisé.

facts.quizQuestion:
Le colis {___} la poste demain matin.
(The parcel will be sent by the post office tomorrow morning.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The parcel will be sent by the post office tomorrow morning.
facts.explanation: sera envoyé par — passif au futur simple de envoyer. Sujet masculin singulier (le colis) → participe passé non accordé : envoyé.

facts.quizQuestion:
La loi {___} le ministre la semaine prochaine.
(The law will be signed by the minister next week.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The law will be signed by the minister next week.
facts.explanation: sera signée par — passif au futur simple de signer. Sujet féminin singulier (la loi) → participe passé accordé : signée.

facts.quizQuestion:
Ces téléphones {___} une usine en Asie.
(These phones are manufactured by a factory in Asia.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: These phones are manufactured by a factory in Asia.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Passif présent — sont + PP (masculin pluriel) + par
facts.explanation: sont fabriqués par — passif présent de fabriquer. Sujet masculin pluriel (ces téléphones) → participe passé accordé : fabriqués.

facts.quizQuestion:
Ces maisons {___} les Romains il y a deux mille ans.
(These houses were built by the Romans two thousand years ago.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: These houses were built by the Romans two thousand years ago.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Passif passé composé — ont été + PP (féminin pluriel) + par
facts.explanation: ont été construites par — passif au passé composé de construire. Sujet féminin pluriel (ces maisons) → participe passé accordé : construites.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il {___} il est fatigué.
(He says that he is tired.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He says that he is tired.
facts.explanation: dit que — discours rapporté au présent avec dire que. Pas de changement de temps à B1 quand le verbe introducteur est au présent.

facts.quizQuestion:
Le professeur {___} la règle est simple.
(The teacher explains that the rule is simple.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The teacher explains that the rule is simple.
facts.explanation: explique que — verbe introducteur expliquer au présent, suivi de que + proposition au présent. Pas de décalage temporel à B1.

facts.quizQuestion:
Elle {___} elle ne sait pas.
(She replies that she doesn't know.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She replies that she doesn't know.
facts.explanation: répond que — verbe introducteur répondre au présent, suivi de que + proposition déclarative.

facts.quizQuestion:
Le témoin {___} il a vu quelqu'un partir.
(The witness states that he saw someone leave.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The witness states that he saw someone leave.
facts.explanation: affirme que — verbe introducteur affirmer au présent. Exprime une assertion forte. Suivi de que + proposition.

facts.quizQuestion:
Elle {___} tu veux venir avec nous.
(She asks whether you want to come with us.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She asks whether you want to come with us.
facts.explanation: demande si — pour rapporter une question fermée (oui/non). Si remplace est-ce que / l'inversion dans le discours indirect.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il {___} elle va arriver à l'heure.
(He wonders whether she will arrive on time.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He wonders whether she will arrive on time.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Discours rapporté — se demander si (doute intérieur)
facts.explanation: se demande si — se demander (verbe pronominal) exprime le doute intérieur. Construit avec si pour les questions oui/non.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il {___} tu fais ce soir.
(He asks what you are doing this evening.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He asks what you are doing this evening.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Discours rapporté — demander ce que (question partielle — objet)
facts.explanation: demande ce que — ce que remplace qu'est-ce que / que dans le discours indirect quand l'objet interrogé est le complément du verbe.

facts.quizQuestion:
Elle {___} se passe dans la salle.
(She asks what is happening in the room.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She asks what is happening in the room.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Discours rapporté — demander ce qui (question partielle — sujet)
facts.explanation: demande ce qui — ce qui remplace qu'est-ce qui dans le discours indirect quand le sujet du verbe est l'élément interrogé.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il {___} elle ne répond pas à ses messages.
(He wonders why she doesn't reply to his messages.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He wonders why she doesn't reply to his messages.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Discours rapporté — se demander pourquoi (doute intérieur)
facts.explanation: se demande pourquoi — exprime une réflexion intérieure sur la cause. Pourquoi reste invariable dans le discours indirect.

facts.quizQuestion:
Elle {___} on peut arriver à la gare.
(She asks how one can get to the station.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She asks how one can get to the station.
facts.explanation: demande comment — comment conserve sa forme dans le discours indirect. Ordre sujet-verbe : comment on peut arriver (pas d'inversion).

facts.quizQuestion:
On {___} l'amour est aveugle.
(They say that love is blind.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: They say that love is blind.
facts.explanation: dit que — on dit que est une expression courante pour rapporter une idée reçue ou un proverbe. Verbe au présent, pas de changement de temps.

facts.quizQuestion:
Elle {___} ses vacances étaient formidables.
(She tells us that her holidays were wonderful.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She tells us that her holidays were wonderful.
facts.explanation: raconte que — verbe introducteur raconter au présent pour relater une expérience. Suivi de que + proposition.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il {___} la réunion commencera à dix heures.
(He adds that the meeting will start at ten o'clock.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He adds that the meeting will start at ten o'clock.
facts.explanation: ajoute que — verbe introducteur ajouter au présent pour apporter une information supplémentaire. Suivi de que + proposition.

facts.quizQuestion:
Le médecin {___} elle a bien dormi.
(The doctor asks whether she slept well.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The doctor asks whether she slept well.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Discours rapporté — demander si (oui/non — contexte médical)
facts.explanation: demande si — question oui/non rapportée. Si remplace est-ce que. L'ordre reste sujet-verbe : si elle a bien dormi.

facts.quizQuestion:
Le guide {___} le château date du douzième siècle.
(The guide explains that the castle dates from the twelfth century.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The guide explains that the castle dates from the twelfth century.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Discours rapporté — expliquer que (présent — fait historique)
facts.explanation: explique que — expliquer que est fréquent dans les contextes informatifs. Le présent historique se maintient dans la subordonnée.

facts.quizQuestion:
L'étudiant {___} il n'a pas encore rendu le devoir.
(The student replies that he has not handed in the homework yet.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The student replies that he has not handed in the homework yet.
facts.explanation: répond que — répondre que introduit une réponse directe. Présent du verbe introducteur → pas de concordance des temps au passé.

facts.quizQuestion:
La journaliste {___} le ministre pense de la nouvelle loi.
(The journalist asks what the minister thinks of the new law.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The journalist asks what the minister thinks of the new law.
facts.grammarPointLabel: Discours rapporté — demander ce que (complément d'objet)
facts.explanation: demande ce que — ce que introduit une question indirecte sur l'objet. Que (direct) → ce que (indirect) : Que pense-t-il ? → demande ce qu'il pense.

facts.quizQuestion:
Elle {___} les trains sont toujours en retard.
(She wonders why trains are always late.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She wonders why trains are always late.
facts.explanation: se demande pourquoi — exprime une réflexion intérieure sur la cause. Pourquoi reste invariable dans le discours indirect.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il {___} apprendre le piano.
(He is starting to learn the piano.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He is starting to learn the piano.
facts.grammarPointLabel: commencer à + inf — Périphrastique de début
facts.explanation: commencer à + infinitif exprime le début d'une action. On dit commencer à (not commencer de) with most verbs.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je {___} comprendre le problème.
(I am starting to understand the problem.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I am starting to understand the problem.
facts.grammarPointLabel: commencer à + inf — Périphrastique de début
facts.explanation: commencer à + infinitif marque le début d'un processus. Tatoeba 13832: « Je commence à comprendre. »

facts.quizQuestion:
Il a plu et les enfants {___} courir.
(It rained and the children started running.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It rained and the children started running.
facts.grammarPointLabel: se mettre à + inf — Périphrastique de début (brusque)
facts.explanation: se mettre à + infinitif indique un début souvent soudain ou inattendu. It implies the start of an action with some abruptness.

facts.quizQuestion:
Elle {___} pleurer quand elle a entendu la nouvelle.
(She started crying when she heard the news.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She started crying when she heard the news.
facts.grammarPointLabel: se mettre à + inf — Périphrastique de début (brusque)
facts.explanation: se mettre à + infinitif conveys a sudden start. Unlike commencer à, it carries a nuance of abruptness.

facts.quizQuestion:
Tu peux sortir quand tu {___} travailler.
(You can go out when you have finished working.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: You can go out when you have finished working.
facts.grammarPointLabel: finir de + inf — Périphrastique de fin
facts.explanation: finir de + infinitif exprime la fin d'une action. It is followed by the infinitive, not a noun.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il a {___} lire son livre avant minuit.
(He finished reading his book before midnight.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He finished reading his book before midnight.
facts.grammarPointLabel: finir de + inf — Périphrastique de fin
facts.explanation: finir de + infinitif = to finish doing something. Compare: finir + noun (finir le livre) vs finir de + infinitif (finir de lire).

facts.quizQuestion:
J'aimerais pouvoir {___} fumer.
(I wish I could give up smoking.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I wish I could give up smoking.
facts.grammarPointLabel: arrêter de + inf — Périphrastique de cessation
facts.explanation: arrêter de + infinitif = to stop doing something. Tatoeba 12555: « J'aimerais pouvoir arrêter de fumer. »

facts.quizQuestion:
La pluie a {___} tomber vers midi.
(The rain stopped falling around noon.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The rain stopped falling around noon.
facts.grammarPointLabel: cesser de + inf — Périphrastique de cessation (formel)
facts.explanation: cesser de + infinitif = to stop doing something. More formal than arrêter de. Il ne cesse de is a common fixed construction meaning 'keeps on doing'.

facts.quizQuestion:
Ne le dérangez pas — il {___} travailler.
(Don't disturb him — he is in the middle of working.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Don't disturb him — he is in the middle of working.
facts.grammarPointLabel: être en train de + inf — Action en cours
facts.explanation: être en train de + infinitif exprime une action en cours au moment où l'on parle. It is stronger than the simple present for emphasizing ongoing activity.

facts.quizQuestion:
Elle {___} préparer le dîner quand je suis rentré.
(She was in the middle of preparing dinner when I got home.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She was in the middle of preparing dinner when I got home.
facts.grammarPointLabel: être en train de + inf — Action en cours (passé)
facts.explanation: être en train de conjugated in the imparfait (était en train de) expresses an ongoing action interrupted in the past.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je {___} le rencontrer — il est encore dans le couloir.
(I just met him — he is still in the hallway.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I just met him — he is still in the hallway.
facts.explanation: venir de + infinitif exprime le passé récent (just did something). Tatoeba 12603: « Je viens de le rencontrer. »

facts.quizQuestion:
Le film {___} commencer — assieds-toi vite !
(The film has just started — sit down quickly!)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The film has just started — sit down quickly!
facts.explanation: venir de + infinitif = to have just done. Present tense of venir de describes something that happened moments ago.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il faut {___} faire des efforts malgré les difficultés.
(We must keep making efforts despite the difficulties.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: We must keep making efforts despite the difficulties.
facts.explanation: continuer à + infinitif (or continuer de, equally correct) = to continue doing something. Both prepositions are acceptable in standard French.

facts.quizQuestion:
Elle {___} partir quand tu as appelé.
(She had just left when you called.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She had just left when you called.
facts.explanation: venait de + infinitif (imparfait of venir de) expresses the recent past in a past context: 'had just done'.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je reste à la maison {___} il pleut.
(I'm staying home because it is raining.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I'm staying home because it is raining.
facts.grammarPointLabel: parce que — Connecteur causal (réponse à pourquoi)
facts.explanation: parce que introduces the direct cause in answer to pourquoi. It is the neutral causal connector and can open or follow a sentence.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} tu es là, aide-moi à porter ces cartons.
(Since you are here, help me carry these boxes.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Since you are here, help me carry these boxes.
facts.explanation: puisque is used when the cause is already known to the listener or considered obvious. Unlike parce que, it cannot be used to answer pourquoi directly.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} il était tard, nous avons décidé de partir.
(As it was late, we decided to leave.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: As it was late, we decided to leave.
facts.grammarPointLabel: comme — Connecteur causal (en début de phrase)
facts.explanation: comme as a causal connector always comes at the beginning of the sentence. It presents the cause as background context rather than new information.

facts.quizQuestion:
Le match a été annulé {___} la pluie.
(The match was cancelled because of the rain.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The match was cancelled because of the rain.
facts.grammarPointLabel: à cause de + nom — Cause négative
facts.explanation: à cause de + noun expresses a cause, often with a negative or undesirable result. Contrast with grâce à which implies a positive result.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il a réussi l'examen {___} son travail acharné.
(He passed the exam thanks to his hard work.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He passed the exam thanks to his hard work.
facts.explanation: grâce à + noun expresses a cause with a positive result. Contrast with à cause de which is used for negative or neutral causes.

facts.quizQuestion:
Le vol a été retardé {___} des conditions météorologiques.
(The flight was delayed due to weather conditions.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The flight was delayed due to weather conditions.
facts.grammarPointLabel: en raison de + nom — Cause formelle
facts.explanation: en raison de + noun is a formal/written equivalent of à cause de. Common in official communications and formal registers.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je n'irai pas à la fête, {___} je suis trop fatigué.
(I won't go to the party, for I am too tired.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I won't go to the party, for I am too tired.
facts.explanation: car is a coordinating conjunction introducing a causal clause. It cannot begin a sentence and is more formal and written than parce que.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} les résultats sont mauvais, nous devons changer de stratégie.
(Given that the results are poor, we must change our strategy.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Given that the results are poor, we must change our strategy.
facts.explanation: étant donné que introduces a cause treated as an established fact. It is formal and typically used to justify a conclusion.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il a travaillé dur, {___} il a réussi.
(He worked hard, so he succeeded.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He worked hard, so he succeeded.
facts.explanation: donc is the most common consecutive connector expressing logical conclusion or consequence. It can appear at the start of the clause or after the verb.

facts.quizQuestion:
Tu n'as pas faim ? {___} ne mange pas.
(You're not hungry? Then don't eat.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: You're not hungry? Then don't eat.
facts.explanation: alors expresses consequence, especially in spoken French. It is more colloquial than donc and often used in if-then reasoning.

facts.quizQuestion:
Les routes sont verglacées ; {___} soyez prudents.
(The roads are icy; consequently, be careful.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: The roads are icy; consequently, be careful.
facts.explanation: par conséquent is a formal consecutive connector meaning 'consequently' or 'as a result'. It is common in formal writing and official registers.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il a plu toute la semaine, {___} les rues sont inondées.
(It rained all week, so that the streets are flooded.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It rained all week, so that the streets are flooded.
facts.explanation: si bien que introduces a result that follows from the preceding statement. It is equivalent to 'so that' or 'with the result that'.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il a révisé chaque jour, {___} il était bien préparé.
(He revised every day, so that he was well prepared.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: He revised every day, so that he was well prepared.
facts.grammarPointLabel: de sorte que + indicatif — Résultat obtenu
facts.explanation: de sorte que + indicatif expresses an actual result (consequence). With subjonctif it expresses purpose — at B1, the indicatif result meaning is the focus.

facts.quizQuestion:
Il faisait {___} froid {___} nous n'avons pas pu sortir.
(It was so cold that we couldn't go out.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: It was so cold that we couldn't go out.
facts.explanation: tellement + adjective/adverb + que expresses a degree of intensity that produces a consequence. Equivalent to 'so…that' in English.

facts.quizQuestion:
Elle a {___} travaillé {___} elle est tombée malade.
(She worked so much that she fell ill.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: She worked so much that she fell ill.
facts.explanation: tant + verb + que expresses 'so much…that'. Compare: tellement (used with adj/adv), tant (used with verbs). Both express degree followed by consequence.

facts.quizQuestion:
Je partirai quand John {___}.
(I will leave when John arrives.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: I will leave when John arrives.
facts.explanation: After quand in future contexts, French uses futur simple — not the present tense as English speakers expect. Cf. Tatoeba 11791: « Je partirai quand John viendra. »

facts.quizQuestion:
Partons quand vous {___}.
(Let's leave when you are ready.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Let's leave when you are ready.
facts.explanation: After quand for a future event, use futur simple. Tatoeba 10301: « Partons quand vous serez prêt. »

facts.quizQuestion:
Dès qu'il {___}, nous pourrons manger.
(As soon as he has finished, we can eat.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: As soon as he has finished, we can eat.
facts.grammarPointLabel: dès que + futur antérieur — Séquence future
facts.explanation: After dès que in future contexts, futur antérieur (aura fini) is used when the subordinate action is completed before the main clause.

facts.quizQuestion:
Appelez-moi {___} vous arriverez à la gare.
(Call me as soon as you arrive at the station.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Call me as soon as you arrive at the station.
facts.explanation: dès que (as soon as) requires futur simple in the subordinate clause when the main clause is in the future or imperative. This parallels quand + futur.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} tu auras terminé tes devoirs, tu pourras jouer.
(Once you have finished your homework, you can play.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: Once you have finished your homework, you can play.
facts.grammarPointLabel: une fois que + futur antérieur — Séquence future
facts.explanation: une fois que (once) also requires futur simple or futur antérieur in French when the main clause refers to a future situation.

facts.quizQuestion:
{___} je serai grand, je serai astronaute.
(When I grow up, I will be an astronaut.)
facts.sentenceTranslation: When I grow up, I will be an astronaut.
facts.explanation: lorsque (when) is a more formal synonym of quand. Like quand, it requires futur simple in the subordinate clause when the main clause refers to the future.

chainThemes.name: Subjonctif Présent
chainThemes.description: Subjonctif présent — regular -er/-ir/-re forms + all irregular paradigms (être, avoir, aller, faire, vouloir, pouvoir, savoir) + trigger expressions (necessity, desire, emotion, purpose, concession, time, condition)

chainThemes.name: Conditionnel & Si-Clauses
chainThemes.description: Conditionnel présent (regular + irregular stems) + si + imparfait → conditionnel (hypothesis) + conditionnel passé intro (j'aurais dû, tu aurais pu, j'aurais aimé)

chainThemes.name: Plus-que-parfait & Temps Composés
chainThemes.description: Plus-que-parfait with avoir (j'avais parlé, il avait pris) and être (j'étais parti(e), elle était arrivée) + temporal sequence (quand j'ai appelé, il était déjà parti)

chainThemes.name: Pronoms Relatifs Avancés
chainThemes.description: Dont (replaces de + noun) / où (place and time) / ce qui (subject) / ce que (object) / ce dont (de + complement) / celui qui / celle que — indefinite and demonstrative relative forms

chainThemes.name: Double Pronominalization
chainThemes.description: Pronoun order: me-te-se-nous-vous < le-la-les < lui-leur < y < en — combinations me le, te la, le lui, les leur, m'en, il y en a + placement with imperatives (donne-le-lui, ne me le dis pas)

chainThemes.name: Gérondif & Participe Présent
chainThemes.description: Gérondif (en + -ant): simultaneity, manner, condition, cause — subject must match main clause / Participe présent (bare -ant): adjectival agreement (souriant, vivant, travaillant) + verbal/literary uses

chainThemes.name: Passif & Discours Rapporté
chainThemes.description: Passif: être (any tense) + PP (agreement with subject) + par + agent / Discours rapporté au présent: dire que, demander si, demander ce que/ce qui, se demander pourquoi (tense unchanged with present reporting verb)

chainThemes.name: Verbes Périphrastiques & Connecteurs Avancés
chainThemes.description: Aspect periphrases: commencer à, se mettre à, finir de, arrêter de, être en train de, venir de + causal connectors (puisque, grâce à, en raison de) + consecutive connectors (si bien que, tellement…que) + quand/dès que + futur

answerTypePools.description: Subjonctif présent regular -er verb conjugations (que je parle, que tu parles, etc.)
Subjonctif présent regular -ir verb conjugations (que je finisse, que tu finisses, etc.)
Subjonctif présent regular -re verb conjugations (que je vende, que tu vendes, etc.)
Subjonctif présent irregular: être (sois, soit, soyons, soyez, soient) and avoir (aie, aies, ait, ayons, ayez, aient)
Subjonctif présent other irregular verbs: aller (aille), faire (fasse), vouloir (veuille), pouvoir (puisse), savoir (sache), falloir (faille), valoir (vaille)
Subjonctif trigger phrases expressing necessity/obligation (followed by que + subjonctif)
Subjonctif trigger phrases expressing desire, emotion, doubt, or concession (followed by que + subjonctif)
Purpose, time, and condition conjunctions requiring subjonctif (pour que, avant que, à condition que, etc.)
Conditionnel présent forms — regular and high-frequency irregular verb forms
Conditionnel passé forms with avoir and être (j'aurais parlé, je serais parti(e), j'aurais dû, tu aurais pu)
Full si + imparfait → conditionnel présent hypothetical constructions (homogeneityExempt — phrase-level variation is inherent)
syntheticDistractors: Si j'avais su, je serais venu.
Si nous partions maintenant, nous arriverions à temps.
Si le temps le permettait, nous sortirions.
Si tu avais de la patience, tu réussirais.
Si on travaillait ensemble, ce serait plus facile.

answerTypePools.description: Plus-que-parfait with avoir auxiliary (avais/avait/avions/avaient + past participle)
Plus-que-parfait with être auxiliary + agreement (j'étais parti(e), elle était arrivée, ils s'étaient couchés)
Relative pronouns dont (replacing de + noun) and où (place or time antecedent) — single-word identification
Indefinite relative pronouns with unspecified antecedent: ce qui (subject), ce que (object), ce dont (de + complement)
Demonstrative relative pronouns: celui qui / celui que / celle qui / celle que / ceux qui / celles que
Double pronoun combinations: me/te/nous/vous + le/la/les (me le donne, te la prête, nous les explique)
Double pronoun combinations: le/la/les + lui/leur — correct order (le lui donne, la leur montre, les leur envoie)
Double pronoun constructions with en and y: m'en, t'en, nous en, y en — (Il m'en a parlé. Il y en a beaucoup.)
Double pronoun placement with affirmative and negative imperatives (Donne-le-lui! / Ne me le dis pas.)
Gérondif forms: en + present participle (en parlant, en faisant, en sortant, en souriant)
Bare participe présent forms used adjectivally (souriant, vivant, obéissant, travaillant) — without en
Passif constructions: être (various tenses) + past participle (agreement with subject) + par + agent
Reporting verb structures for discours rapporté au présent: dire que, demander si, demander ce que, se demander pourquoi, expliquer que
Beginning and end aspect periphrases: commencer à, se mettre à, finir de, arrêter de, cesser de + infinitive
Ongoing and recent past aspect periphrases: être en train de, venir de, continuer à/de + infinitive
Causal connectors: parce que, puisque, comme, à cause de, grâce à, en raison de, faute de — nuance distinctions
Consecutive/result connectors: donc, alors, c'est pourquoi, si bien que, de sorte que, tellement…que, par conséquent
Temporal conjunctions that require futur simple in French (not present tense as in English): quand, dès que, une fois que, aussitôt que, lorsque
